seoTitle: How to Add Grammarly to Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: Install the Grammarly browser extension to get real-time grammar, spelling, and tone suggestions directly inside Google Docs.
intro: Catching grammar mistakes and awkward phrasing is faster with Grammarly running right inside Google Docs, instead of copying text back and forth. This guide covers installing the browser extension and using its inline suggestions.

steps.heading: Open your browser's extension store
steps.body: Launch the Chrome Web Store or your browser's equivalent extension catalog and find the search field.

steps.heading: Install Grammarly
steps.body: Search for Grammarly, select the verified listing, and click 'Add to Chrome' to begin installation.

steps.heading: Sign in to your account
steps.body: Follow the redirect to log into your Grammarly account, or create one if you don't already have it.

steps.heading: Open a Google Doc
steps.body: Load any document and look for the floating Grammarly widget that appears in the bottom right corner.

steps.heading: Review and apply suggestions
steps.body: Click underlined text to see grammar, spelling, and clarity suggestions, then accept the ones you want to apply.

faq.question: Is Grammarly free to use with Google Docs?
faq.answer: A free plan covers basic grammar and spelling checks, while advanced tone and clarity features require a paid subscription.

faq.question: Does Grammarly work on Google Docs mobile?
faq.answer: Browser extension features are limited to desktop, but Grammarly also has its own mobile keyboard app.

faq.question: Can I adjust the writing tone Grammarly checks for?
faq.answer: Yes, the widget hub lets you set goals like audience, formality, and intent to tailor its suggestions.

faq.question: Will Grammarly slow down my document?
faq.answer: It shouldn't noticeably affect performance for typical documents, though very large files may see a slight lag.
